schema.title: Response List Resources Api V1 Resources Get
Response List Events Api V1 Events Get

delete.description:
Delete an entire recurring series.
Only valid for recurring root events.
For cancelling a single occurrence use POST /{id}/cancel-occurrence.
For cancelling a one-off event use POST /{id}/cancel.

schema.title: Response List Bookings Api V1 Bookings Get
Response List Users Api V1 Users Get

get.description:
Free/busy status of every active resource on `date`.

Powers the room-list colour dots (`is_free`) and the empty-room search filter.

schema.title: Response Day Availability Api V1 Availability Day Get

get.description:
Open windows on one resource for `date`, at least `duration_minutes` long,
searched inside the working window [from_hour, to_hour).

schema.title: Response List Groups Api V1 Groups Get
Response List Roster Api V1 Roster Get

post.description: Check clashes for a *proposed* event (used by the create form before saving).

schema.title: Response Preview Clashes Api V1 Clashes Preview Post

get.description:
Clashes for an event. Pass start/end to preview at a NEW time (used when editing).
Student-clash detail is host-only (privacy rule E).

schema.title: Response Event Clashes Api V1 Clashes Event  Event Id  Get

get.description: Requests where I am the holder (someone wants my slot).

schema.title: Response Incoming Api V1 Release Requests Incoming Get
Response Outgoing Api V1 Release Requests Outgoing Get

BookingStatus.title: BookingStatus
BookingStatus.description:
Finite state machine for booking lifecycle.

PENDING → APPROVED or REJECTED (if requires_approval)
PENDING → CONFIRMED (if auto-approve)
CONFIRMED → CANCELLED
APPROVED → CONFIRMED → CANCELLED

Why model as enum FSM rather than boolean flags?
- States are mutually exclusive
- Transitions are explicit (enforced in service layer)
- Future states (WAITLISTED, NEGOTIATING) slot in cleanly

ProposedEvent.title: ProposedEvent
ProposedEvent.description:
What the requester wants — either CREATE a new event, or MOVE an existing one
(move_event_id) into the freed slot. Captured so accept can fulfil it.

ResourceType.title: ResourceType
ResourceType.description:
Why enum and not a free-form string?
Controlled vocabulary prevents drift ("Lab" vs "lab" vs "Laboratory").
New types added here — never hardcoded in business logic.

VenueBooking.title: VenueBooking
VenueBooking.description: Who currently holds a contested room (meeting C: 'see who holds a booked slot').
